ucs.title: [LLM] Daggerfly (Evasive Panda) MgBot DLL side-loading via legitimate signed host process + rundll32 loader
ucs.description: Hunts for the signature MgBot/Nightdoor execution chain Daggerfly has used since 2014: a legitimately-signed binary (often Tencent QQ, Kaspersky, or McAfee components) sideloads an attacker DLL from its install directory, or rundll32 invokes a sideloaded module by its known MgBot export. Anchors on the unique combination of trusted parent + non-Microsoft DLL outside C:\Windows\System32 + outbound HTTP to a non-corporate destination.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=rundll32.exe AND (Processes.process="*hex.dll*" OR Processes.process="*libcef.dll*" OR Processes.process="*BdaPlugin.dll*" OR Processes.process="*Acrobat.dll*" OR Processes.process="*data.dat*" OR Processes.process="*,InitializeAddress*" OR Processes.process="*,EntryPoint*")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("QQ.exe","QQProtect.exe","TXPlatform.exe","avp.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","PptCore.exe","RsMgrSvc.exe") AND Processes.process_name!=Processes.parent_process_name AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Program Files*" AND Processes.process_path!="*\\Windows\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as net_conns values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080) AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | where net_conns>0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let SuspectExports = dynamic(["InitializeAddress","EntryPoint","DllRegisterServer","StartW","RunDLL"]);
let MgBotHostBinaries = dynamic(["qq.exe","qqprotect.exe","txplatform.exe","avp.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","pptcore.exe","rsmgrsvc.exe","360se.exe"]);
let RunDll = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("hex.dll","libcef.dll","BdaPlugin.dll","Acrobat.dll","data.dat","hex.bin")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex strcat(",\\s*(", strcat_array(SuspectExports,"|"), ")\\b");
let SideLoad = DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (MgBotHostBinaries)
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
| where (isempty(SHA256) or Signer == "" or SignatureType == "Unsigned")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ImgFolder=FolderPath, ImgFile=FileName, Host=InitiatingProcessFileName, HostHash=InitiatingProcessSHA256, Pid=InitiatingProcessId;
union RunDll, SideLoad
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemotePort in (80,443,8080)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | summarize C2Hosts=make_set(RemoteUrl,25), C2IPs=make_set(RemoteIP,25), Conns=count() by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
) on DeviceId
| where Conns > 0
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=tostring(AccountName), FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, ImgFile, ImgFolder, Host, C2Hosts, C2IPs, Conns
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Daggerfly is famously the EXCLUSIVE operator of MgBot, and Symantec/ESET reporting shows the loader chain repeatedly hijacks Tencent QQ, Kaspersky avp.exe, and McAfee mcoemcpy.exe to side-load hex.dll/libcef.dll/BdaPlugin.dll, often invoked via rundll32 with the export 'InitializeAddress'. Combining the named host-binary list, the named MgBot DLL filenames, the suspicious export, and immediate egr

ucs.title: [LLM] Daggerfly software-update supply-chain hijack — legitimate updater fetching unsigned payloads (Tencent QQ / Adobe / Sogou)
ucs.description: Hunts Daggerfly's signature initial-access vector: AitM/abuse of legitimate software update channels (Tencent QQ updater, Sogou Pinyin, Adobe Acrobat updater) to deliver MgBot/Nightdoor to NGO, telco, and dissident targets in Asia/Africa. Looks for known updater processes writing executables to disk from non-vendor IPs, then spawning rundll32 or a child binary that beacons.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped_file from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name IN ("TXPlatform.exe","QQPCRTP.exe","QQUpdate.exe","AdobeARM.exe","AdobeUpdateService.exe","PinyinUp.exe","SGTool.exe","WPSUpdate.exe","360se.exe") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bin") AND Filesystem.file_path!="*\\Program Files\\*" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as updater_ip values(All_Traffic.dest) as updater_host from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("TXPlatform.exe","QQUpdate.exe","AdobeARM.exe","AdobeUpdateService.exe","PinyinUp.exe","SGTool.exe") AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest="*tencent.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*qq.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*adobe.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*sogou.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*kingsoft.com") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as child_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("TXPlatform.exe","QQPCRTP.exe","AdobeARM.exe","PinyinUp.exe","SGTool.exe") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe") OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\AppData\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | rename Processes.dest as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let Updaters = dynamic(["txplatform.exe","qqpcrtp.exe","qqupdate.exe","adobearm.exe","adobeupdateservice.exe","pinyinup.exe","sgtool.exe","wpsupdate.exe","360se.exe"]);
let VendorDomains = dynamic(["tencent.com","qq.com","adobe.com","sogou.com","kingsoft.com","360safe.com"]);
let Drops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Updaters)
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName endswith ".bin"
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, Updater=InitiatingProcessFileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedSHA256=SHA256;
let Net = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Updaters)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| extend Apex = tostring(split(RemoteUrl,"/")[2])
| where not(Apex has_any (VendorDomains))
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, Updater=InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP;
let Children = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Updaters)
| where FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
   or FolderPath has_any (@"\Users\",@"\AppData\",@"\ProgramData\")
| project ChildTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, Updater=InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProc=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
Drops
| join kind=inner Net on DeviceId
| join kind=inner Children on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', DropTime, NetTime)) <= 30
  and abs(datetime_diff('minute', DropTime, ChildTime)) <= 30
| project DropTime, DeviceName, Updater, DroppedPath, DroppedFile, DroppedSHA256, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, ChildProc, ChildCmd
| sort by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: ESET (2023) and Symantec (2024) attribute multiple intrusions to Daggerfly via hijacked update flows of Tencent QQ, Sogou Pinyin, and other PRC-popular software — a near-unique signature given the actor's NGO/Tibetan-diaspora targeting in Asia/Africa. Joining (a) updater process drops outside its install path, (b) updater traffic to a non-vendor apex domain, and (c) the updater spawning a LOLBin/A
